categories.title: Haven Perks — Use Every One
tips: Priority embarkation — arrive 10:30am, skip the main line
Haven sun deck (Deck 18) has a private pool, hot tub, and bar — far less crowded than main pool
Haven Restaurant (Deck 17) open all day — no reservations needed, dedicated staff
Butler handles in-suite dining, unpacking requests, specialty coffee setup
Haven Concierge books shows and specialty dining — ask day 1
Priority disembarkation — concierge arranges, no waiting in main terminal

categories.title: Formal Nights
tips: April 5 (Sea Day / Anniversary) — formal or smart casual
April 8 (Costa Maya day) — second formal night
Haven Restaurant is always appropriate; specialty restaurants also observe dress code

categories.title: What's Included in Haven
tips: All Haven Restaurant meals
Butler and concierge service
3 complimentary bottles from the spirits list (choose at check-in)
Thermal Suite week pass (already booked)
Priority access to shows and venues
Haven-exclusive sundeck and pool

categories.title: Extra Charges to Know
tips: Specialty restaurants (Onda, Los Lobos) — charged per person
In-room energy drinks — charged even with beverage package (confirm package covers)
Spa treatments beyond thermal suite
Casino, shore excursions, gratuities beyond standard

categories.title: Butler Tips
tips: Introduce yourself and share preferences on day 1
Request morning coffee/tea setup time
Ask for extra pillows, robes, or room setup changes
Tip butler and concierge separately at end of cruise (~$20-30/day for butler, $10-15/day for concierge is standard Haven)
